name: Mrs. Suszczynski's Molasses Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Melt the shortening. Stir in the sugar and molasses. Add the egg, beating very well. Mix in the baking soda, flour, ground cloves, ground ginger, and ground cinnamon until everything is well combined. Chill the dough for a few minutes. Shape the dough into small walnut-sized balls and roll them in sugar. Place the cookies about 2 inches apart on a greased baking sheet. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 to 12 minutes. Let the cookies cool for 2 minutes before removing them from the baking sheet. Allow them to finish cooling on a rack.
Recipe from Mrs. Jerry Suszczynski of Glendora, CA. Formatted by Lynn Thomas. Source: Los Angeles Times, November 21, 1996. Makes about 3 dozen. Each cookie contains approximately: 90 calories; 3 mg sodium; 6 mg cholesterol; 4 grams fat; 12 grams carbohydrates; 1 gram protein; 0.02 gram fiber.
